commands.review_summary:
The PR moves Codex workspace persona and memory files from turn-scoped or prompt-context inputs into thread-level developer instructions and updates related docs, tests, and prompt snapshots.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests -6, Docs 0. Total 0 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main keeps SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md, and USER.md out of inherited thread instructions while MEMORY.md remains in turn prompt context, and the PR diff changes both surfaces. I did not run a live Codex channel/subagent repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This needs maintainer judgment on the Codex-native subagent inheritance contract plus contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: Keep Codex workspace persona session scoped
target.body:
## Summary

This cleans up the Codex persona regression from #85811 without doing a blind revert of unrelated prompt lifecycle work.

Before #85811, the stable OpenClaw identity files were no longer being copied into the model input every Discord or Telegram turn. #85811 moved `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md` into turn-scoped Codex collaboration instructions so Codex-native subagents would not inherit the main agent persona. That solved the wrong problem and brought the bloat back: every normal channel turn carried the same stable persona context again.

This puts the stable workspace identity back where it belongs: once on the Codex thread as developer instructions. Normal user turns no longer replay those files through collaboration-mode instructions. Heartbeat-specific guidance stays turn-scoped, and `MEMORY.md` remains turn context. I also made the thread-level workspace identity survive `before_prompt_build` hooks that replace the Codex system prompt, so plugin prompt hooks cannot accidentally strip the session persona.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: normal Codex channel turns no longer replay stable workspace persona files in turn-scoped collaboration instructions; `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `USER.md` are attached to the Codex thread developer instructions instead.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree on macOS, branch `codex/codex-session-persona-context`, head `bc4ebb2354221c2a84251e0e2e8f6607d4c23e48`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`; `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`; `oxfmt --check --threads=1` over the touched code, docs, and snapshot files; `git diff --check`; local autoreview with web search disabled.

Evidence after fix: the Codex run-attempt test now asserts the stable workspace identity files appear in `thread/start.developerInstructions`, do not appear in `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`, and are preserved even when `before_prompt_build` replaces the system prompt. The Discord and Telegram prompt snapshots now show no normal-turn persona replay in collaboration-mode instructions.

Observed result after fix: the focused Vitest run passed 243 tests, prompt snapshots were current, formatting and whitespace checks passed, and autoreview reported no accepted or actionable findings.

What was not tested: no live Discord agent was restarted from this branch in Pash's running dev environment.
target.author: pashpashpash
